meta.pageTitle: Open Framework for Artists · AI Pathways Map

siteHeader.brandSubtitle: An open framework for protection, care, and experimentation

hero.title: Becoming Digital Ancestors
hero.bodyBeforeProject: We will all be ancestors one day, so what does it mean to be a digital ancestor? Take the quiz and find out which character you are, then learn about what your path might look like by checking out our resources.

why.title: Why we care
why.body: Generative AI is reshaping how art is made, circulated, and valued. It can bring up grief, frustration, curiosity, and possibility all at the same time. This project has brought together practicing artists, AI researchers, technologists, and policy advisors who care about artist autonomy and responsible technology. We've created a space for learning and sharing in an everchanging landscape.

storyCard.intro: This map grew out of months of creative research and development experiments that we titled
storyCard.middle: . It started with a question: Can AI help care for creative legacy, and cultural memory? We began to imagine futures where digital ecosystems coexisted with nature and humans and from this designed gallery works, kept process journals, and tested a number of tools over six months. We hosted an art exhibit sharing our imagined futures and turned our learnings into an
storyCard.middleSuffix: that other artists can adapt, critique, and help build upon.
storyCard.closing: Use this website to clarify what you might want from your own engagement with AI, from opting out entirely to collaborating on new work.

who.title: Arts communities and the people around them
who.body: The framework is designed for working artists and creative practitioners. It is also useful for cultural workers, arts organizations, educators, policy-makers, and AI builders who want to better understand artist needs.

artists.heading: For artists and creative professionals
artists.body: Clarify your boundaries, learn practical safety steps, and explore new workflows if you want them. Use the map when posting work, signing agreements, applying for funding, or testing new tools.

organisations.heading: For arts and legal organisations
organisations.body: Use the framework in workshops, office hours, or resource hubs. The map can support legal clinics, digital safety training, and collective discussions.

policy.heading: For policy makers
policy.body: See where artists encounter friction in current tools and contracts. Use the map to surface real-world scenarios when shaping policy.

researchers.heading: For AI researchers and developers
researchers.body: Treat the map as a set of user stories that highlight artist concerns around consent, provenance, and creative control.

cards.label: Steward
cards.title: Office Siren
items: Favourite Medium: All of it, just no AI art please

map.title: Take the Quiz and Find Out Which Character You Are

resources.title: All pathway resources
resources.body: Every link surfaced in the interactive map, organized by pathway and the step where it appears.

siteFooter.outro: project in collaboration with artists and responsible AI practitioners. This is a living framework (v23).
